# UC-14.3.58 · Aranet Wireless Sensor RSSI Degradation and Mesh Margin

## Description

Aranet LoRa sensors at the edge of RF coverage produce intermittent gaps before they go fully offline. Tracking RSSI or vendor signal quality percentage predicts dropped CO₂/temperature samples in cleanrooms, labs, and cold chains.

## Value

Stops blind spots in regulated environmental monitoring by moving or repeating gateways before sensors mute.

## Implementation

When ingesting Aranet JSON, map `rssi` from the gateway payload (dBm) or compute `signal_quality_pct` if the broker exposes a 0–100 score. Poll at the same cadence as telemetry (5–15 min). Alert when RSSI is weaker than −115 dBm (tune per antenna) or `signal_quality_pct` < 30 for two consecutive intervals. Correlate with new metal racking or relocated chillers.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Stable `sensor_id` key shared with CMMS.
• If only CSV export exists, use `inputcsv` batch jobs until streaming lands.

Step 1 — Normalize RSSI field name across Aranet4 vs PRO lines.

Step 2 — Baseline winter vs summer RF noise.

Step 3 — Validate by walking sensors away from gateway in lab.

Step 4 — Auto-task facilities when >5 sensors breach.

## SPL

```spl
index=environment sourcetype=aranet:sensor sensor_id=*
| stats latest(rssi_dbm) as rssi, latest(signal_quality_pct) as sigq by sensor_id, location
| where rssi < -115 OR sigq < 30
| table sensor_id, location, rssi, sigq
| sort rssi
```

## Visualization

Choropleth or floorplan heatmap (RSSI), Table (worst sensors), Line chart (RSSI trend).
